summary.intent: Fix asset naming for date-based release versions (e.g. 20260327.01) in install.sh and release.yml, then cut a new release to validate the fix.
summary.outcome: Released 20260327.02 with corrected deb/rpm naming via cargo flags and updated URL construction in both root and website install.sh copies.

repo: Release tags use bare date strings (e.g. 20260327.01) rather than semver v-prefixed tags; install.sh needed a release_tag() helper to handle both formats.
website/install.sh is a separate file (not a symlink) that serves https://www.moltis.org/install.sh and must be manually synced with the root install.sh.
cargo deb requires --deb-version flag and cargo generate-rpm requires --set-metadata='version=...' to override the Cargo.toml version for artifact naming.

code.finding: get_latest_version() sed regex updated to handle both semver (v0.10.18) and date-based (20260327.01) tag formats; release_tag() helper added to conditionally prefix v for semver tags.
Stale copy of install.sh that was out of sync; must be updated in lockstep with root install.sh on every change.
cargo deb and cargo generate-rpm invocations needed explicit version flags to produce correctly named artifacts when MOLTIS_VERSION differs from Cargo.toml version.

workflow: YubiKey confirmation is required for authenticated git push operations, causing push to block until tapped.
After fixing release tooling, cutting an immediate follow-up release (patch .02) is necessary to validate the fix since the prior release (.01) shipped with the broken naming.

friction: YubiKey tap requirement interrupted the push flow and required a manual retry.
website/install.sh being a non-symlink duplicate is a latent sync hazard — nearly shipped a release with a stale install script.
The 20260327.01 release shipped with incorrect deb/rpm artifact names because the fix wasn't in place yet, requiring an immediate patch release.
open_items: Consider making website/install.sh a symlink or CI-enforced copy to prevent future drift.
The 20260327.01 release has permanently incorrect deb/rpm naming and may confuse users who try to install it manually.
